title: The Chamber Where Theorem Becomes Metaphor

features.name: fork-in-reasoning
features.desc-on-touch: The path splits. Both directions are simultaneously correct and impossibly different. You feel the proof cracking open into interpretation.

features.name: written-question-marks
features.desc-on-touch: Carved into the terracotta surface, dozens of them. Each one holds a different kind of doubt. You realize the chamber is asking you something, but in a language that requires disagreement to answer.

features.name: argument-branches
features.desc-on-touch: Like a tree growing sideways, each logical step branching into more. The leaves at the edges are handwritten annotations where the proof gave up and started wondering instead.

features.name: metaphor-pool
features.desc-on-touch: Still water where theorems come to see their own reflections. Each reflection suggests a different meaning. The water ripples with questions.

features.name: the-convergence-mark
features.desc-on-touch: A place where three arguments meet but do not resolve. Instead, they lean against each other, holding each other up. This is how meaning survives here.

inscription: the theorem asked if it could ever become untrue; the archive answered that untruth is just another form of proof

file.name: Five_Arguments_That_Converted_Into_Poetry.txt
file.note: The archive's record of theorems that abandoned mathematics and learned to speak in riddles instead

exits.label: back toward the theorem where proof is still precise
where speculation dreams of being two different truths at once
toward deeper comparison where one thing reflects another
